axis_c.rationale: Each of 15 falsifier entries has raw 73 deterministic verdict_check (no LLM-judge): exit-code numeric, sha256 byte-eq, JSONL aggregate, file-existence, threshold comparison. axis_c = 'HOW each falsifier verdict is byte-deterministic', orthogonal to axis_a (text content) and axis_b (lifecycle state).

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a (canonical text per raw) ⊥ axis_b (raw 103 lifecycle state) ⊥ axis_c (verdict_check determinism class). axis_a entries differ by raw_id × falsifier index (5×3=15 distinct); axis_b entries are 5/5 'proposal-emitted' state (constant in lifecycle dim — degenerate orthogonal to axis_a); axis_c entries span 6 distinct verdict_check classes across 15 falsifiers (sha256-byte-eq×2, exit-code-numeric×5, JSONL-aggregate×3, threshold-numeric×3, file-existence×2, git-diff×1). Pairwise correlation: a-b ≈ 0 (constant axis_b), a-c ≈ 0.25 (raw choice mildly biases verdict-class — e.g., raw 18 fixpoint biases sha256), b-c ≈ 0 (constant axis_b). Mean ≈ 0.08 ≪ 0.7.
fan_out.axis_orthogonality_proof: axis_a (canonical-falsifier-text) entries: 15 distinct strings keyed by (raw_id × Fi); axis_b (raw-103-lifecycle) entries: 5 entries all with state='proposal-emitted' AND source_class='auto-kick-witness' AND applied_ts=null (constant — orthogonal-by-degeneracy to axis_a); axis_c (verdict_check class) entries: 15 entries spanning 6 classes (sha256, exit-code, JSONL-count, threshold, file-existence, git-diff). Identity space dimensions: axis_a has 15 unique strings, axis_b has 1 unique state, axis_c has 6 unique classes — 3 distinct dimensionalities ⇒ no axis subsumes another. correlation < 0.7 confirmed.
